reviews.review_msg:
-midterm and homework heavy
-strong non-understandable accent
-doesnt know how to explain content which made learning hard no one came to class due to this
-class was very hard thought i was doing good before the final
-final worth 45% (class average 38%) didnt curve it filled with content never taught, grammar errors and lots of incoherences

Overall, the class was pretty difficult. Joan's lectures did not particularly help, unfortunately due to her strong accent and the overwhelming amount of material. With around 25+ chapters cramming is not option. However, thanks the curve and pop quizzes throughout the semester I survived.

I thought I was smart before taking this class. Joan is such a sweet lady but by the end of the term I was convinced she was manipulating me. Her final was a psychological game, nothing like any of the homework or even the midterm. If you take this class I would also recommend you start praying to a god.

I am not kidding when I say professors who cannot speak a lick of English should teach heavy courses like this. The final exam (worth 45% of the grade) was filled with content never taught, grammatical errors, and countless incoherences which made the class so much harder. I had no idea what was being asked of me in any of the exams.

I would say I have more of a stats background then most of the people who take this course which was the only reason I passed (many of my friends did not). Prof does not care about your learning. Slides were terrible, I almost completely relied on the textbook which was not enough to do well on the exams.

Theres three midterms, just did one for which the content was EASY, except we had 50 minutes and 25 questions, so 2 minutes per question. Class average for this was 13/22 and she said that it was good. Ridiculous to take a class who's content is easy and make it nearly impossible to do by not giving students enough time - just give us the class.

I took 305 and Joan as a person is very kind, and sweet but as a professor honestly she is terrible.  Avoid this course with her at all costs.  Her lectures make no sense, even though she spends the entire time speaking none of it makes sense.  Tests are ridiculously difficult I failed this course the first time, and squeezed a C second time around

*not for stat 201, but 305 - she is the sweetest, nicest, kindest and most passionate stat prof, however she is terrible at explaining concepts and you have to teach urself for the whole course. 3 midterms, started off super easy then got progressively worse and the exams are entirely written w no mc. hw is all r code that u have to teach urself

Joan has a genuine excitement for statistics that I find to be contagious beyond the classroom. Her assignments helped me to engage with the material and challenged me exactly the right amount. She also happens to be a great person that will be in your corner if you demonstrate interest and effort in her class.
